rulings.Case Title: THOMAS HIXSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 28, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Albay Construction Company¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to 2 and 3 (causation), granted as to issue 4 (strict liability) and taken off calendar as to issue 5 (punitive damages). Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing fireproofing disturbed by defendant. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration are overruled. Plaintiff appears to concede the lack of merit to its claim for strict liability. Plaintiff says that he will dismiss his request for punitive damages. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS HIXSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS HIXSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 28, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Anderson, Rowe & Buckley, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to negligence and premises owner/contractor liability and off calendar as to strict liability, false representation and punitive damages. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials disturbed by defendant. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony precludes defendant from shifting the burden. Plaintiff has dismissed his claims for strict liability, false representation and punitive damages. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS HIXSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 28, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Scott Co. of California¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing materials disturbed by defendant. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony precludes defendant from shifting the burden. (See, e.g., Hixson depo at 1454.) Even if defendant had sustained its burden, plaintiff¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing materials disturbed by defendant. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS HIXSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 28, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Flowserve Corporation, SII to DURAMETALLIC CORPORATION, INCORRECTLY NAMED AS DURAMETALLIC CORPORATION¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to negligence and strict liability (issues 1 and 2) and taken off calendar as to false representation and punitive damages (issues 3-5). Mr. Westbrook¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing Durametallic packing. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Westbrook¿s declaration are overruled. Plaintiff has dismissed his claims for false representation and punitive damages. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS HIXSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 28, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Perini Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing material disturbed by defendant. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony precludes defendant from shifting the burden. (See Hixson depo at 1739:13-25, 1741:6-12.) Even if defendant sustained its burden, plaintiff¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing materials disturbed by defendant. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS HIXSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 28, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Bayer Cropscience, Inc. SUCCESSOR TO AMCHEM¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Plaintiff¿s declaration and defendant¿s responses to General Order 129 interrogatories create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to an asbestos-containing product (Foster¿s 81-93 fibrous adhesive) attributable to defendant. Plaintiff¿s objections to Mr. Sage¿s declaration are sustained. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration are overruled. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS HIXSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS HIXSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 28, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant National Steel and Shipbuilding Company¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony precludes defendant from sustaining its burden. Even if defendant had sustained its burden, plaintiff¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing insulation that was original to ships built by defendant. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LARRY VERWORN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, APRIL 28, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 7. Defendant Kelly-Moore Paint Co., Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to negligence, strict liability, false representation and civil battery and granted as to punitive damages. As to negligence and strict liability, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s purported undisputed fact no. 13 acknowledges that plaintiff¿s amended responses to special interrogatories identified co-worker Henry Graham and stated what knowledge Mr. Graham had about plaintiff¿s work with Paco products. A reading of plaintiff¿s response to the cited interrogatories shows that defendant left out of its separate statement some information known by Mr. Graham that was identified in plaintiff¿s response. Further, defendant¿s separate statement failed to include facts and evidence demonstrating that Mr. Graham does not have such knowledge. There is no evidence provided by Mr. Graham¿s deposition testimony from his own case that he was ever specifically asked about plaintiff. Even if defendant had sustained its burden of production, the declarations of Mr. Graham and Mr. Hawkins and defendant¿s responses to General Order No. 129 interrogatories create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to the declarations of Mr. Graham and Mr. Hawkins are overruled. Defendant failed to submit evidence in its separate statement that made false representation and civil battery issues, other than whether plaintiff established that he worked with defendant¿s product. As to punitive damages, defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiff failed to submit clear and

rulings.Case Title: LARRY VERWORN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, APRIL 28, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 7. Defendant Union Carbide Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LARRY VERWORN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, APRIL 28, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 7. Intervenors¿ motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 and 2 (negligence and strict liability) and off calendar as to issues 3, 4 and 5 (false representation, civil battery and punitive damages). Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s purported undisputed fact nos. 12 and 13 acknowledge that plaintiff¿s amended responses to special interrogatories identified co-worker Bob Hawkins and stated what knowledge Mr. Hawkins had about plaintiff¿s work with Rich-Tex products. Defendant¿s separate statement failed to include facts and evidence demonstrating that Mr. Hawkins does not have such knowledge. Plaintiff has dismissed his claims for false representation, civil battery and punitive damages. = (220/HEK)
